summary:
The delay (>6 hours) before developing respiratory issues supports an alternate diagnosis other than early-onset Respiratory Distress Syndrome for a neonate who was otherwise considered mature at birth.
```

The summary focuses only on the critical point that late onset of symptoms rules out RDS more likely indicating some condition unrelated to classic RDS seen shortly post-birth among premature infants. ```plaintext Summary:

A term infant showing signs later than 6 hours indicates conditions beyond classical Respiratory Distress Syndrome; hence, options suggesting prompt or antecedent factors are less supportive.
```
